mnemonics: Every Vehicle Comes Packed: Gas, Ether, Wallet, Smart Contracts (Ethereum Virtual Machine, Gas, Ether, Wallet, Smart Contracts)
keyPoints: Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM): The global, decentralized computer that executes all smart contracts.
Gas: The unit that measures the computational effort required to execute operations, paid for in Ether.
Ether (ETH): The native cryptocurrency used to pay for transaction fees and computational services.
Wallets: Software that stores private keys and interacts with the blockchain to manage Ether and deploy contracts.
Smart Contracts: Self-executing code deployed on the blockchain that automatically enforces the terms of an agreement.
commonMistakes: Thinking Gas and Ether are the same thing. Gas is the unit of work; Ether is the currency used to pay for it.
Believing wallets store cryptocurrency. Wallets store keys; the coins exist on the blockchain ledger.
Assuming all blockchain data is stored on every node. Light clients only store block headers, not the entire state history.
